metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for this branch

bypass.reason: Internal dispatch module called by bridges, not a drone-routable command module
Internal dispatch module, not discoverable via drone @hooks
Engine has its own JSONL diagnostic logging, does not use branch json_handler
Bridges are designed to import engine module — that is their entire purpose
Bridge is a thin entry point, no JSON operations to log
Drone-routed entry point, output via sys.stdout.write to drone subprocess capture, not CLI
Drone-routed entry point, --version handled in main(), not a standalone CLI binary
Test fixtures live in tests/, not in the 3-layer apps structure
Test files live in tests/, not in the 3-layer apps structure
Test methods use descriptive names as documentation per pytest convention
Test data contains command references as part of test fixtures, not user-facing help
Bridge called from provider settings (external subprocess), not internal imports
Bridge intentionally imports engine module — bridges exist to call the engine
Bridge imports engine module by design — that is its sole purpose
print_introspection() called by drone's discovery system, not internal code
Bridge called from provider settings (external subprocess), not imported internally
Bridge called externally by provider settings subprocess — no internal import
Hooks branch does not use json_handler — has its own JSONL logging
Hooks has no json_handler create_default/save_invalid/invalid_mode patterns

notes.usage: Add entries to bypass specific seedgo standard violations

fields.standard: Which standard to bypass (imports, cli, naming, etc.)
